questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Consider the following individuals who have been awarded the Pulitzer Prize:

options.text: They are all recipients in the category of Investigative Reporting.
They are all Indian photojournalists who have won the prize.
They are all members of the Ghadar Party based in America.
They are all recipients of the Padma Bhushan for literature.

questions.explanation: The individuals listed—Adnan Abidi, Sanna Irshad Mattoo, Amit Dave, and Mukhtar Khan—are specifically identified in the source as photojournalists who have won the Pulitzer Prize.
questions.takeaway: A significant number of recent Indian Pulitzer winners have been recognized in the field of photojournalism.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: ...and photojournalists Danish Siddiqui, Adnan Abidi, Sanna Irshad Mattoo, Amit Dave, Dar Yasin, and Mukhtar Khan.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Which of the following prominent members of the Ghadar Party in America became the first Indian to win the Pulitzer Prize?
questions.explanation: Gobind Behari Lal, a member of the Ghadar Party in America and a postgraduate from the University of California, Berkeley, became the first Indian to win a Pulitzer Prize in 1937.
questions.takeaway: Gobind Behari Lal combined his association with the Ghadar Party with a distinguished career in journalism in the United States, ultimately winning the Pulitzer Prize for reporting.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: In 1937, Gobind Behari Lal became the first Indian to win a Pulitzer... A member of the Ghadar Party in America, Gobind Behari Lal was a postgraduate from University of California, Berkeley.
